articles.title: Periscope Equity Makes Strategic Investment in Amusement Connect Payments Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Equity has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a specialty payments platform built for the amusement industry. The infusion of capital signals growing investor confidence in purpose-built fintech solutions for FEC operators. This deal could accelerate product development and broader adoption of integrated payment tools across arcades, FECs, and related venues.

articles.title: ROLLER Partners with Adyen to Offer Embedded Financing to 3,000+ Venues
articles.summary: ROLLER, a leading venue management platform for FECs and attractions, has partnered with global payments giant Adyen to provide fast, embedded financing through a Capital product available to its 3,000-plus venue customers. The integration lets operators access working capital directly through their existing ROLLER platform, reducing friction for growth investments. This is a significant development for FEC operators looking for flexible funding options tied to their operational data.

articles.title: Pico Play to Debut PlayMAXX RFID Gameplay System at IAAPA Expo Asia
articles.summary: Pico Play will unveil PlayMAXX, an RFID wristband-based gameplay system designed for FECs and theme parks, at IAAPA Expo Asia in Hong Kong this June. The system enables guests to compete, earn achievements, and personalize their experience across multiple attractions within a venue. FEC operators looking to deepen engagement and differentiate their offerings should take note of this scalable, quest-driven platform.

articles.title: Funlandia's PlayMaze Brings Dynamic Mechanical Interactivity to Indoor FECs
articles.summary: Funlandia has developed PlayMaze, a next-generation indoor maze product featuring moving pathways, airflow effects, light, and motion-reactive obstacles designed to differentiate FECs in an increasingly crowded market. The product addresses the growing challenge of venue sameness, where basic soft play and climbing structures no longer set operators apart. PlayMaze will be showcased at IAAPA Expo Asia, making it a timely consideration for operators planning new builds or refreshes.

articles.title: TicketSpice Selected as Ticketing Platform for All Eight Enchanted Parks Locations
articles.summary: TicketSpice has been chosen to power admissions for all eight Enchanted Parks properties, which were acquired in the recent $330 million Six Flags asset sale. The deal underscores TicketSpice's growing footprint in the parks and attractions space as a flexible, operator-friendly ticketing solution. FEC operators evaluating ticketing platforms will want to watch how TicketSpice scales across multiple large venues.

articles.title: K1 Speed Opens Multi-Level Indoor Go-Kart Center in Thornton, Colorado
articles.summary: K1 Speed has opened a new indoor amusement center in Thornton, Colorado, featuring a multi-level go-kart track as its centerpiece attraction. The expansion continues K1 Speed's aggressive national growth strategy for indoor karting venues. For FEC operators and investors, the opening reinforces strong consumer demand for experiential indoor entertainment anchored by motorsport-style attractions.

articles.title: AI Is Transforming Guest Experiences at Theme Parks and Attractions
articles.summary: Major operators including Six Flags, Disney, and Universal are deploying AI across guest-facing and operational functions, from digital concierges and personalized experiences to safety systems and interactive wearables. The article provides a broad overview of how AI is reshaping visitor engagement and park operations at scale. FEC operators should monitor these developments as the technology becomes more accessible and expected by guests.

articles.title: Intercard to Showcase Award-Winning iQ QR Code Reader at IAAPA Expo Asia
articles.summary: Intercard will present its iQ cashless reader, which supports QR code transactions, at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026 in Hong Kong. The iQ reader is positioned as a key tool for FEC operators seeking to modernize cashless payment infrastructure across game cards and attractions. Operators attending the expo or evaluating cashless systems will want to review Intercard's latest hardware offering.

articles.title: Activate Expands Nordic Footprint with First Denmark Location
articles.summary: Activate, the competitive gaming and active entertainment brand, is opening its first location in Denmark, continuing its rapid international expansion across Europe. The concept, which blends physical activity with technology-driven games, is gaining traction as a differentiated FEC format. Operators tracking competitive socializing and active gaming trends should watch Activate's growth as a potential model or competitive benchmark.

articles.title: IP Licensing Strategy: The Structural Decision That Shapes Every Immersive Project
articles.summary: A new analysis explores how the choice to license established intellectual property for immersive attractions is not just a creative decision but a structural one, with significant contractual, financial, and operational implications. Approval rights, usage restrictions, and revenue sharing can deeply affect project timelines and profitability. FEC operators and developers considering IP-based experiences should carefully weigh these factors before committing to licensed content.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Center Leases Former Sears Space at Sunrise Mall
articles.summary: An indoor entertainment center has signed a lease for part of the former Sears anchor space at Sunrise Mall, reflecting the ongoing trend of FEC concepts backfilling large retail vacancies. This type of adaptive reuse continues to be a major growth driver for the indoor entertainment industry as mall owners seek experiential tenants to replace departed department stores. Operators and developers should note the leasing activity as a signal of continued retail-to-FEC conversion momentum.

articles.title: Spokane Valley FEC Flight 509 Closes Permanently After Two Years
articles.summary: Flight 509, a family entertainment center in Spokane Valley, Washington, has permanently closed its doors after just two years in operation, drawing disappointment from local families ahead of summer break. The closure highlights the operational challenges facing newer FEC entrants, particularly around sustaining foot traffic beyond the initial opening buzz. This story serves as a cautionary case study for operators on the importance of long-term programming and retention strategies.

articles.title: Six Flags Fiesta Texas Opens Werewolf Gorge, Billed as World's Longest Family Launch Coaster
articles.summary: Six Flags Fiesta Texas has opened Werewolf Gorge, a record-setting family launch roller coaster featuring four launches, a mid-course rollback, and immersive storytelling with a 39-inch height requirement. The attraction is positioned as a multi-generational experience, expanding the park's appeal beyond traditional thrill-seekers. The investment reflects Six Flags' broader strategy of adding family-accessible attractions to drive incremental visitation and per-cap spending.

articles.title: IAAPA Expo Asia 2026 Preview: Education, Innovation, and Networking in Hong Kong
articles.summary: IAAPA Expo Asia 2026 runs June 9-12 at the Hong Kong Convention and Exhibition Centre, featuring over 20 EDUSessions on major industry trends alongside product debuts and networking events. The Asia-Pacific region is seeing significant investment in new attractions, making this expo a key venue for global operators to benchmark and source ideas. FEC professionals planning international expansion or seeking new products and partners should consider this event a priority.

articles.title: H. Betti Industries Names Michael Arnone Senior Director of Marketing
articles.summary: H. Betti Industries, a major amusement industry supplier based in New Jersey, has appointed Michael Arnone as Senior Director of Marketing, bringing over 20 years of brand strategy and digital transformation experience to the role. The hire signals a renewed focus on marketing and customer engagement at one of the industry's key equipment and supply companies. FEC operators who work with H. Betti will likely see updated communications and partnership initiatives in the coming months.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Center Opens in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: Thunderzone Family Entertainment has opened a new location in Carrollton, Georgia, adding another FEC option to a market that continues to see new venue development. The opening reflects ongoing demand for local family entertainment options in mid-sized markets across the Southeast. Operators and investors tracking regional FEC growth will want to monitor how Thunderzone performs in this competitive environment.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Planned for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center is in development for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee, a fast-growing suburb east of Nashville. The announcement adds to a wave of FEC development in high-growth suburban markets across the Sun Belt. Operators and developers watching suburban market expansion will find this a useful data point for site selection analysis.

articles.title: Immotion Expands Immersive VR Platform to Science Centers and Museums
articles.summary: Immotion, which has deployed motion-based VR experiences at more than 150 zoo and aquarium locations, is expanding its platform to science centers and museums seeking to boost engagement and dwell time. The company's 360-degree cinematic VR with motion seating places guests inside natural and prehistoric environments in a way that supports education and repeat visitation. FEC operators considering VR attractions should evaluate Immotion's proven revenue-share or lease models as a lower-risk entry point.

articles.title: Play CENLA Family Entertainment Center Shuts Down in Louisiana
articles.summary: Play CENLA, a family entertainment center in Louisiana's Rapides Parish, has closed permanently, adding to a pattern of FEC closures that underscore the sector's operational challenges. The shutdown follows a broader trend of smaller FEC operators struggling to sustain profitability in competitive local markets. Operators and investors should treat this as another data point when assessing market saturation and business model resilience.

articles.title: The Twisty Economics of Amusement Parks: Revenue Models Explained
articles.summary: A new analysis examines the complex and often counterintuitive economics driving amusement park profitability, including pricing strategies, per-cap revenue, and the balance between admission and in-park spending. The piece offers insights relevant to FEC operators who face similar economic trade-offs at a smaller scale, particularly around membership programs and food and beverage revenue. Understanding these dynamics can help FEC operators benchmark their own financial models against larger industry players.

articles.title: Kids Bowl Free Summer Program Expands Across Kentucky and Southern Indiana
articles.summary: Multiple bowling centers across Kentucky and Southern Indiana are participating in the Kids Bowl Free summer program, offering complimentary lanes to children to drive family traffic during the summer months. The program is a well-established customer acquisition and traffic-building tool for bowling centers looking to introduce young guests and their families to the sport. FEC operators with bowling components should evaluate participation in similar programs to maximize summer attendance.

articles.title: Menasha's Sabre Lanes Bowling Center Closes, Redirects Customers to Appleton Location
articles.summary: Sabre Lanes in Menasha, Wisconsin has permanently closed, with the ownership directing displaced bowlers to their Appleton bowling center. The closure reflects ongoing consolidation pressure on standalone bowling centers facing rising costs and evolving entertainment competition. FEC operators with bowling as a core offering should monitor similar closures as indicators of market health and potential acquisition opportunities.

articles.title: Dorney Park Donates 4,000+ Tickets for Annual Community Day of Giving
articles.summary: Dorney Park & Wildwater Kingdom in Allentown, Pennsylvania is welcoming more than 4,000 children and family members from local nonprofits as part of its annual Day of Giving initiative. The program provides complimentary admission and special experiences to underserved families, strengthening the park's community ties and brand goodwill. FEC operators looking to build local relationships and generate positive PR should consider similar community partnership programs.

articles.title: Masti Zone Expands Indian FEC Footprint with New Lucknow Location
articles.summary: Masti Zone, an indoor family entertainment brand, has opened a new location in Lucknow, India, reflecting the rapid growth of the FEC sector in the country's tier-two cities. India's expanding middle class and appetite for family-oriented indoor entertainment are driving significant venue investment across the subcontinent. Global FEC suppliers and investors tracking emerging market opportunities should note India's accelerating pace of new venue development.

articles.title: London-Based Interactive Entertainment Concept Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A London-origin indoor entertainment space combining foosball, virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is set to open in Paris, signaling the cross-border expansion of competitive socializing and interactive entertainment concepts in Europe. The format reflects the growing global appetite for adult-oriented FEC experiences that blend gaming, food, and beverage. U.S. FEC operators should watch European competitive socializing models as a source of product and programming inspiration.

articles.title: Southern California Birthday Party Search Demand Signals Opportunity for FEC Operators
articles.summary: A GlobeNewswire release highlights that Southern California families are actively searching for birthday party venues, representing a significant marketing opportunity for local FEC operators in one of the country's largest family entertainment markets. Birthday party packages remain one of the highest-margin revenue streams for FECs, and capturing search-driven intent is critical to conversion. Operators in the region should evaluate their digital marketing and SEO strategies to capitalize on this demand.

articles.title: Dellshire Resort Opens in Wisconsin Dells with Signature Fire-Breathing Dragon Attraction
articles.summary: Dellshire Resort has opened in Wisconsin Dells, anchored by a large fire-breathing dragon as its signature visual landmark and featuring a range of family entertainment amenities. The opening adds a new experiential resort concept to one of the Midwest's most established family tourism destinations. FEC operators and resort developers interested in destination-style family entertainment will want to track Dellshire's early performance and guest reception.
